message:
feat(stdlib): add setmode + getmode (BSD chmod symbolic mode) (bd-ovcrt)

BSD setmode(mode_str) parses a chmod-style symbolic mode string
("u+x", "g-w", "a=rwx", "u=rwx,go=rx") into an opaque "bitbox" of
operations; getmode(bbox, current_mode) applies it to current_mode.
Used by chmod(1), find(1) -perm, install(1), mtree, and many other
BSD utilities. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi.

* core::stdlib::setmode::parse returns Vec<ChmodOp> with kind
  (Add/Clear/Set), who_mask, and bits already filtered to who.
  Handles comma-chained clauses, default-to-`a` empty who, multiple
  op-perm pairs in one clause (e.g. "u+x-w"), and r/w/x/s/t perms.
  Strict on unknown chars and missing op-perm pairs.
* core::stdlib::setmode::apply walks the ops applying Add (|=),
  Clear (&!), or Set (clear who_mask, then OR bits).
* stdlib_abi::setmode allocates a [magic header][ChmodOp] buffer on
  the C heap so getmode can recover the slice from an opaque void*.
  Returns NULL+EINVAL on parse error or NULL input. Caller frees
  the bitbox.
* stdlib_abi::getmode validates the magic header (NULL or wrong
  magic returns the input unchanged — defensive on caller-supplied
  buffers) before applying.

Not yet supported (returns None): the X conditional perm, copy-from
u/g/o-as-perm. Both are documented BSD extensions but rarely seen.

23 core unit tests covering: every who/op/perm combination, comma
chaining, multiple op-perm in a clause, setuid/setgid/sticky,
high-file-type-bit preservation, malformed inputs (empty, unknown
char, missing op, trailing comma, garbage between clauses) + 11
abi integration tests covering NULL safety, EINVAL mapping, magic
header validation, multi-clause round trip. Clippy-clean,
fmt-clean. All builds via rch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
